tracks.meaning: to make a hole through something by drilling, piercing, or tunneling
a hole made in the earth during drilling for oil, water, or other resources
a person or thing that is tediously dull or uninteresting
to cause someone to feel weary or uninterested through prolonged dullness or repetition
the internal diameter of a gun barrel or cannon
to hit a hole through something, especially in golf when a ball goes directly into the hole
the path made by a parasitic insect or worm through the skin or flesh of an animal
a hole or tunnel-like passage through body tissue
to push forward steadily and forcefully through resistance
the diameter of a hole, especially in machinery or manufacturing
